question: From the words lettered A to D, choose the word that has the same vowel sound as the one represented by the underlined letters or letters underlined. Brought
solution: The word ‘bought’ is transcribed /bc:t/ and it is the long vowel sound of /Ɔ/. The answer is A ‘roar’ /rc:r/ or /rc:r/ and it is the long vowel sound as ‘bought’ /bc:t/. the others have different vowel sounds like ‘plough’ /plau/, which has the diphthong /au/, ‘drought’ /draut/, also with the diphthong /au/ and ‘worse’ /wƷ:s/ which has a different sound.

question: In three words, describe the principal ( Mr. Mallum) as described in the novel.

option.a: small, wiry and spoke through his nose
option.b: fat, smooth-cheeked and loved his job

section: This question is from the novel 'The Last Days at Forcados High school'.
solution: In chapter two, we were meant to understand that Mr.Mallum was a small, wiry man with an odd accent, as if he spoke through his nose.

question: It was a funny episode, I ----------------
section: From the options, choose the word(s) that best filll the gap.
solution:
The correct answer is (C) because a sentence is arrange in a formation which is (subject, verb and object) the subject is the doer of the action , the verb is the action (auxiliary + lexical/ main verb) and the object which is the receiver of the action.
I couldn’t help laughing = past continuous tense
Subject (I) auxiliary verb (couldn’t) hexical verb (help)
The other options are disarranged and not appropriate

question:
From the words lettered A to D, choose the word that rhymes with the given word.

Breeze
solution: The answer is ‘D’ because only ‘trees’ /tri:z/ whose plural form ‘-s’ becomes ‘-z’ rhymes with ‘breeze’ /bri:z/. The other options ‘stress’ and ‘chess’ have similar rhymes, leaving free /fri:/ alone

question: <u>Z</u>oo

question:
Choose the option that best fills the gap.

Most of his personal ---------------stolen
solution: The correct answer is ‘A’ ‘effects were’ because the word ‘effects in this context  means belongings / luggages or properties. The word ‘defect’ means ‘a fault or malfunction. The other options are totally incorrect.

question:
From the words lettered A to D, choose the word that has the same vowel sound as the one represented by the underlined letters or letters underlined.

gran<u>i</u>te
solution: The answer is B because the word ‘sit’ has the same vowel sound as the one underlined in ‘granite. The letter ‘I’in granite represent the short vowel. Sound /i/ and not the diphthong /ai/. Granite /graenit/ not /grænait/ /sit/ for sit.

question:
From the words lettered A to D, choose the word that has the same vowel sound as the one represented by the underlined letters or letters underlined.

P<u>ur</u>se
solution:
The answer as regards the word purse /pƷ:s/ is journey /dzƷ:ni/ because it has the same vowel sound 'uh'.

‘journey’ is the answer as opposed to the other options; pays \ˈpā\. heart /ha:t/ which has the long vowel /a:/ and course /kc:s/

question: Each of the contestants --------------- a chance now
section: From the options, choose the word(s) that best filll the gap.
solution: The answer is ‘A’ has because it signifies form. According to the law of concord ‘each’ signifies ‘are’ and it has to go with a singular verb and not plural

question:
This question is from the novel 'The Last Days at Forcados High school'.

Who was the teacher to whom the students called “cane”?
solution: The novel described Teacher Bade as “cane” because he was always waiting for the next helpless kid who will fail into his trap

question:
From the words lettered A to D, choose the word that has the same vowel sound as the one represented by the underlined letters or letters underlined.

Sh<u>oe</u>
solution: The answer is B because ‘food’ /fu:d/ has exactly the same vowel sound as ‘shoe’ /su:l/ and it is the long vowel sound of full. The other words in the options. ‘blood’ /blnd/, ‘full’ /ful/ (short form of /u:/ and course /’put’ /put/.

question: Select the wrongly spelt words
solution: The correct answer is ‘B’ ‘beleive’ which is properly spelt as ‘believe’. The others on the list are properly spelt

question: ‘Jims, we have a prefect meeting with Mr Mallum now’ who made this statement?
section: This question is from the novel 'The Last Days at Forcados High school'.
solution:
This statement was made by the headboy, Seyi Lawal when he was asked to call Jimi for a meeting by the principal, Mr Mallum.

See page 9

question: <u>Ch</u>alet
section: From the words lettered A to D, choose the word that has the same consonant sounds as the one represented by the letters underlined.
solution: The answer is ‘C’ because only’ champagne’ /ʃæmpeIn/ has the same constant sound as ‘chalet’ ʃæl.eI/.
